Total Cholesterol | 2021-07-04 | 3015548 | 185

clinical_criteria: Allergy test positive if lab result >= 0.35 kU/L for allergen
ALLERGY_TEST_FLAG = 1 if COUNT_POSITIVE_ALLERGY >= 2 OR 1 COUNT_POSITIVE_ALLERGY and 1 COUNT_POSITIVE_DISTINCT_ICD_Allergen_Group OR COUNT_POSITIVE_DISTINCT_ICD_Allergen_Group >= 2
Eczema or wheezing flag = 1 if diagnoses on 2 separate dates and one diagnosis is on or before age 3
HDL-C cutoffs for quality control: <5 or >200 mg/dL
Total cholesterol cutoffs for quality control: <50 or >1000 mg/dL
Systolic BP cutoffs for quality control: <60 mmHg or >240 mmHg
Unit conversion for total cholesterol and HDL: multiply mmol/L by 38.6 to get mg/dL
Unit conversion for triglycerides: multiply mmol/L by 88 to get mg/dL
algorithm_summary: This phenotype algorithm is designed to support GIRA (Genomic Information Returns and Reports) generation for eMERGE participants. It focuses on collecting clinical data elements for asthma risk prediction in young children using a validated algorithm that considers: (1) allergy test results with positive threshold >= 0.35 kU/L, (2) ICD codes for food allergies and allergen groups, (3) eczema/wheezing diagnoses before age 3 requiring 2 separate diagnostic dates with at least one occurring by age 3, and (4) laboratory values including lipid profiles, blood pressure, and HbA1c. The algorithm creates flags for positive allergy tests and early childhood conditions, while collecting the most recent lab values relative to participant enrollment date. Quality control measures include value range checks and unit conversions for international units.
